one_time_description: Williams Treaty distribution to members recorded under Land Management segment expenses
economic_notes: Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership gaming revenue received. Williams Treaty settlement funds held. Economic development includes resource centre, cottage and small business centre rentals. Flooding claims loan agreements totaling $1,666,148 with Federal Government.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). New accounting standards adopted in 2023: PS3450 Financial Instruments, PS3041 Portfolio Investments, PS2601 Foreign Currency Translation, PS3280 Asset Retirement Obligations. Adjustment on adoption of financial instruments standard resulted in $3,010,630 increase to accumulated remeasurement gains. Asset retirement obligation of $795,000 recognized for asbestos removal in buildings. Williams Treaty distribution to members of $9,585,182 is a very large one-time expense recorded in Land Management segment causing a segment deficit of $7,110,473. Trust funds of $2,294,645 excluded from financial statements. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Restricted cash includes $41,832,476 Williams Treaty internally restricted funds. expenses_salaries_total calculated by summing salaries from all segments: Admin $1,050,757 + Community Ops $579,822 + Economic Dev $500,084 + Education $2,762,624 + Land Mgmt $210,755 + Social Dev $208,842 + Health $1,493,084 = $6,805,968 (Gaming and Cultural not shown in extracted text).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
